display.purpose: Score numbers, candidate name headers, report titles. The serif voice belongs to the product's judgements.

headline.purpose: Panel section headers: Score breakdown, Matched skills, AI summary.

title.purpose: Candidate names in sidebar, column headers, button labels, role selector.

body.purpose: AI summary paragraphs, alert text, description copy. Max 65–75ch.

label.purpose: VERDICT badge text, sidebar section metadata, score denominators. Never decorative eyebrows.

shadows.name: accent-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary button only. Coral-tinted warm glow. Not used anywhere else.

shadows.name: interactive-lift
shadows.purpose: Cards and candidate cards on :hover / :focus-visible only. Never at rest.

shadows.name: float
shadows.purpose: Floating elements: context menu, tooltip, dropdown popover.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transitions (button hover, sidebar hover). 150ms.

motion.name: ease-reveal
motion.purpose: Score ring and bar entrance animations. 600–800ms. Ease-out exponential.

motion.name: score-ring-duration
motion.purpose: Score ring stroke-dashoffset animation from 0 to final value on mount.

motion.name: bar-stagger
motion.purpose: Stagger delay between each score bar entrance in the breakdown section.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action. Coral fill, accent glow shadow, opacity drop on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #f25c54; color: #fff; font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 14px; border: none; border-radius: 11px; padding: 0.6rem 1rem; width: 100%; box-shadow: 0 3px 10px rgba(242,92,84,.32); cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f25c54; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: #f0eee6; border: 1px solid #e3e1d8; color: #c2c4c8; box-shadow: none; cursor: not-allowed; opacity: 1; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Supporting action. White fill, border stroke, no shadow.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; color: #52575f; font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 13.5px; border: 1px solid #e3e1d8; border-radius: 11px; padding: 0.6rem 1rem; width: 100%; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #cfcdc2; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f25c54; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Verdict Badge — HIRE
components.description: Affirmative verdict pill. Always text + dot together.
components.css: .ds-verdict { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; padding: 7px 15px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-verdict-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 99px; display: inline-block; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-verdict-hire { background: #e4efd2; color: #4d6b22; } .ds-verdict-hire .ds-verdict-dot { background: #5f8b2e; }

components.name: Skill Pill — Matched
components.description: Confirmed skill presence. Green checkmark prefix.
components.css: .ds-skill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 7px 14px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13.5px; font-weight: 600; margin: 3px; } .ds-skill-matched { background: #e4efd2; color: #4d6b22; } .ds-skill-missing { background: #f8dcd7; color: #b23a30; }

components.name: Skill Pill — Missing
components.description: Absent required skill. No prefix — absence is the signal.

components.name: Candidate Card (Sidebar)
components.description: 48px sidebar row. Avatar initials + name/role + score. Interactive lift on hover. Right-click triggers delete context menu.
components.html:
JD
Jane Doe
Data Analyst
87
components.css: .ds-candidate-card { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 11px; padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 10px; background: transparent; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; } .ds-candidate-card:hover { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.04); box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); } .ds-candidate-card.active { background: #fdecea; } .ds-candidate-avatar { width: 32px; height: 32px; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 9px; background: #f0eee6; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; color: #6b7078; } .ds-candidate-meta { flex: 1; min-width: 0; } .ds-candidate-name { font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #22272f; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; } .ds-candidate-role { font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; color: #a7a99f; } .ds-candidate-score { font-family: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 700; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field. Coral focus ring matches primary action color.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; } .ds-input-label { font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; color: #22272f; } .ds-input { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #ecebe3; border-radius: 11px; padding: 0.55rem 0.85rem; font-family: 'Source Sans 3', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #22272f; width: 100%; transition: border-color 0.15s, box-shadow 0.15s; outline: none; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #7c818b; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #f25c54; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(242,92,84,.1); } .ds-input.error { border-color: #e84a45; }

narrative.overview:
TalentScan feels like advice from a trusted colleague — someone who's read the CV so you don't have to, and will give you a straight answer. The interface is warm without being casual, professional without being corporate. It doesn't hedge: HIRE is green and means yes, REJECT is red and means no, and the interface is built around the confidence to say so clearly.

The aesthetic is informed by Linear's opinionated precision (every element earns its place, zero decoration) and Notion's structured density (information is a feature, not a liability). The canvas is a warm cream (#faf9f5), not the sterile white of a legacy ATS. Cards surface on clean white. Depth is tonal, not shadowed — surfaces step up in lightness. The only shadow that exists is on the primary button's coral glow, and on interactive elements at hover.

The coral accent (#f25c54) is Coral Confidence: warm enough to avoid alarm, authoritative enough to command attention. It appears on primary actions, active nav states, and the brand mark. Its rarity is deliberate.
keyCharacteristics: Warm cream canvas with white card surfaces — tonal depth without shadows at rest
Coral accent used sparingly: CTAs, active states, brand only
Serif display font (Source Serif 4) for scores, verdicts, and section headers — authority
Sans-serif body (Source Sans 3) for UI copy, labels, metadata — efficiency
Three semantic verdict colors (green/amber/red) always paired with text labels
Score ring is the signature component — the single most important number on results screens

rules.name: The Rarity Rule
rules.body: Coral Confidence appears on ≤15% of any screen. If a surface reads 'coral' when you squint, it's wrong — it should read 'cream with one clear signal.'

rules.name: The Verdict Completeness Rule
rules.body: Verdict colors never appear without a paired text label (HIRE / MAYBE / REJECT). Color alone fails WCAG 1.4.1.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Source Serif 4 speaks for the product's judgements (scores, verdicts, section titles, brand mark). Source Sans 3 handles everything the user operates. Never cross the streams.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: No surface has a shadow at rest unless it's floating (menu, tooltip) or branded (coral button glow). Shadow on hover is feedback, not decoration.

dos: Do use Source Serif 4 for all scores, verdicts, and section headings
Do pair every verdict color badge with a visible text label
Do keep Coral Confidence to ≤15% of any screen
Do use tonal layering (canvas → white card) for depth rather than shadows at rest
Do animate the score ring and bars on first render; respect prefers-reduced-motion
Do use position: fixed or the Popover API for all floating UI
Do keep body text and placeholders at ≥ #7c818b for WCAG AA contrast
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text + gradient)
Don't add colored border-left stripes to cards or list items
Don't build UI that looks like Greenhouse or Workday — dense grey forms, deep nested dropdowns
Don't reference Canva or playful consumer aesthetics — pastel gradients, bubbly rounded corners, confetti
Don't use the generic AI SaaS dashboard aesthetic: big hero metric cards, identical icon-heading-text grids, coral as a dominant surface color
Don't add shadow at rest to non-floating, non-branded surfaces
Don't use uppercase tracked labels as decorative eyebrows on every section
Don't disable states with opacity: 0.5 — use the explicit disabled color set
